title: thymus serpyllum proles polytrichus
intro: Thymus serpyllum proles polytrichus, also known as creeping thyme, is a low-growing, spreading perennial herb native to Europe, with tiny leaves and small, pink or purple flowers. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and antiviral properties, making it a popular remedy for colds, coughs, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of creeping thyme include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Creeping thyme can be prepared as an herbal tea, essential oil, or tincture, and is often used in aromatherapy and natural remedies due to its calming and uplifting effects.
